entities.name: CONSERVATION AGRICULTURE
entities.description: A system of agricultural practices combining minimum tillage, soil cover, and crop diversification to build resilience and improve productivity

entities.name: RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of an agricultural system to continue functioning and providing ecosystem services following an external shock

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A period of deficient rainfall that acts as an external shock to agricultural systems

entities.name: FOOD PROVISIONING
entities.description: An essential ecosystem service provided by resilient agricultural systems

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output, which is expected to be higher in resilient systems during drought

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to sufficient food, improved by conservation agriculture during poor rainfall

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: The amount of precipitation, which when poor, negatively impacts productivity and food security

entities.name: SOUTHERN AFRICA
entities.description: The region where conservation agriculture practices are being promoted and studied

entities.name: EL NINO DROUGHT
entities.description: A specific drought event occurring in 2015/16 in southern Africa

entities.name: MINIMUM OR REDUCED TILLAGE
entities.description: One of the three basic practices of conservation agriculture involving reduced soil disturbance

entities.name: MAINTENANCE OF SOIL SURFACE COVER
entities.description: One of the three basic practices of conservation agriculture using crop residues to cover the soil

entities.name: CROP RESIDUES
entities.description: Plant materials used to maintain soil surface cover in conservation agriculture

entities.name: CROP DIVERSIFICATION
entities.description: One of the three basic practices of conservation agriculture using crop rotations

entities.name: ROTATIONS
entities.description: The practice of alternating crops to achieve diversification

entities.name: CLIMATE SMART AGRICULTURAL PRACTICE
entities.description: A classification of conservation agriculture due to its productivity, resilience, and mitigation benefits

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: The long-term shift in weather patterns that conservation agriculture helps cropping systems resist

entities.name: GREENHOUSE GASES
entities.description: Atmospheric gases that conservation agriculture helps to mitigate

entities.name: MANUAL CA
entities.description: A version of conservation agriculture based on digging planting basins, common among smallholder farmers

entities.name: PLANTING BASINS
entities.description: A manual technique used to implement conservation agriculture

entities.name: MECHANISED CA
entities.description: A version of conservation agriculture utilizing specialized machinery

entities.name: SOIL RIPPERS
entities.description: Equipment used in mechanised conservation agriculture to reduce tillage

entities.name: DIRECT SEEDERS
entities.description: Equipment used in mechanised conservation agriculture for planting

entities.name: JAB PLANTERS
entities.description: Equipment used in mechanised conservation agriculture for planting
